title: The U.S. and Iran are in talks to end the war. Here’s where things stand.
summary: The truce is still holding, but every hard question is still unsolved.

title: U.S. Oil Is Skipping the Chance to Grab Market Share From the Gulf
summary: U.S. oil production is expected to grow only modestly next year as companies hesitate to spend more in an uncertain market.

title: Keir Starmer Resigns as UK Prime Minister
summary: His departure in the coming weeks clears a path for Andy Burnham, a popular Labour Party mayor, to become the country’s seventh prime minister in a decade.

title: Who Is Andy Burnham, the Man Who Could Be Britain’s Next Prime Minister?
summary: Charismatic, northern and exuding a relaxed optimism, Mr. Burnham is a contrast to Keir Starmer. His allies hope he could mend Labour’s relationship with voters.

title: Supreme Court Restores Conviction in 1979 Murder of Etan Patz
summary: Pedro Hernandez was convicted in 2017 of kidnapping and murdering the 6-year-old boy, but an appeals court in July had said Mr. Hernandez was entitled to a new trial.

title: Why Older People Are Divorcing More Than They Used To
summary: Rates of gray divorce have risen sharply over the past few decades — and experts have a few theories as to why.

title: Clive Davis, Music Industry Titan Who Signed Whitney Houston, Dies at 94
summary: He rose from a midlevel position at Columbia Records to become one of music’s most powerful executives, shepherding stars like Barry Manilow and Whitney Houston.

title: 11 Essential Songs Shepherded by Clive Davis
summary: The label boss, who died on Monday, had a passion for hits — especially ones he masterminded — during his formidable tenures at Columbia, Arista and J Records.

title: Barry Manilow, Bruce Springsteen and Others Mourn Clive Davis
summary: Davis “treated me with the same respect and kindness as a 22-year-old nobody as he did after all my success,” Springsteen said after the music executive’s death on Monday.

title: Congressional Democrats Warn Pulte Against Mass Firings
summary: Current and former officials say the acting director of national intelligence is planning to announce major cuts to his office as early as Monday.

title: Judge Finds Justice Dept. Overreach in Subpoenas to Minnesota Officials
summary: The judge quashed the subpoenas, writing that they were designed to “harass and retaliate against” Democratic officials who bucked the Trump administration on immigration enforcement.

title: Justice Department Makes It Easier to Bypass Pollution Controls on Pickups
summary: It has stopped criminal prosecutions of people who install “defeat devices,” which make diesel trucks faster and more efficient but also dirtier.

title: Interior Department Moves to Relax Rules for Drilling on Public Lands
summary: A pair of proposals from the Trump administration would lower costs and weaken requirements for fossil fuel companies that drill on federal property.

title: Alan Greenspan, Fed Chairman Through Prosperity and Crisis, Dies at 100
summary: The pre-eminent economic policymaker of his time and a skilled political operator, he favored market-friendly stances that would later come to be associated with destructive financial forces.

title: In Alan Greenspan, Kevin Warsh Saw a Fed Chairman Role Model
summary: Kevin M. Warsh has long singled out Alan Greenspan, who died on Monday, as the central banker he wants to exemplify.

title: He Provided Security to 3 Governors. Then He Snapped.
summary: Lt. Ricardo Santos of the New Jersey State Police had faced questions about his judgment and conduct before he became the primary suspect in a double murder-suicide.

title: Shootout Suspect Is Dead After Killing Montreal Police Officer, Authorities Say
summary: Fady Dagher, the city’s police chief, said that the “immediate threat to the public is no longer present” and that sheltering in place was no longer required.

title: How Remote Work Has Helped a Generation of Working Parents
summary: Post-pandemic, a new openness to accommodating family needs has made it possible for more mothers and fathers to balance work and parenting — particularly mothers of young children.

title: YouTube Stars Take Center Stage at Creative Artists Agency
summary: For years, creators were on the fringes at Creative Artists Agency, a Hollywood talent behemoth. Now the agency is putting them center stage.

title: Netflix Buys a ‘Hot Ones’ Spinoff
summary: The streaming giant bought a spinoff of a popular online interview series that features celebrities eating fiery chicken wings, the latest salvo in its rivalry with YouTube.

title: Flight of the Conchords and the Perils of Reunions
summary: Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement are routinely asked about performing their musical satires again. They always said no, until this year. What changed?

title: Ten Years After Brexit, the Dismal Verdict Is In
summary: Brexit was supposed to let Britain return to a time when it still counted as a global power. A decade later, the costs are blindingly apparent.

title: The Moment Alan Greenspan Should Be Remembered For
summary: A crucial episode in the famous Fed chair’s legacy.

title: Do You Find the ‘Tradwife’ Lifestyle Intriguing? Tell Us Why.
summary: For women who are newly interested in traditional gender roles and lifestyles: What’s making you feel this way?

title: A Loophole Brings Cystic Fibrosis Patients a ‘Miracle Drug’ in Generic Form
summary: A generic version of a breakthrough cystic fibrosis drug, manufactured in Bangladesh for a fraction of the American price, may give some families around the world an unlikely lifeline.
